zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Pincerna le Boteler於大約1070年出生在英國爾波爾頓 在 普爾福德時，他的父親Roger "Pincerna" D'Aubigny De St. Martin-Aubigny36歲，母親Adeliza De Mowbray27歲。他和Margot Avaranches至少有2個兒子。他去世於1110年之後在他的家鄉，埋葬在英國爾波爾頓 在 普爾福德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard Pincerna le Boteler于大约1070年出生在英国爾波爾頓 在 普爾福德时，他的父亲Roger "Pincerna" D'Aubigny De St. Martin-Aubigny36岁，母亲Adeliza De Mowbray27岁。他和Margot Avaranches至少有2个儿子。他去世于1110年之后在他的家乡，埋葬在英国爾波爾頓 在 普爾福德。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Boteler
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a maker of leather bottles, from an agent derivative of Middle English botel, Old French bouteille ‘bottle’.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Domesday Book
historicEvents.description: Domesday Book is Britain's earliest extant public record with information containing land surveys and landholdings commissioned by William I. The book was completed in 1086.

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Pincerna le Boteler was born about 1070, in Poulton in Pulford, Cheshire, England, his father, Roger "Pincerna" D'Aubigny De St. Martin-Aubigny, was 36 and his mother, Adeliza De Mowbray, was 27. He had at least 2 sons with Margot Avaranches. He died after 1110, in his hometown, and was buried in Poulton in Pulford, Cheshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard Pincerna le Boteler est né environ 1070, à Poulton in Pulford, Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, Roger "Pincerna" D'Aubigny De St. Martin-Aubigny, avait 36 ans et sa mère, Adeliza De Mowbray, avait 27 ans. Il a eu au moins 2 fils avec Margot Avaranches. Il est décédé après 1110, dans sa ville natale, et a été enterré à Poulton in Pulford, Cheshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard Pincerna le Boteler um 1070 in Poulton in Pulford, Cheshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Roger "Pincerna" D'Aubigny De St. Martin-Aubigny 36 und seine Mutter Adeliza De Mowbray 27. Er hatte mit Margot Avaranches mindestens 2 Söhne. Er starb nach 1110 in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Poulton in Pulford, Cheshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Pincerna le Boteler è nato il circa 1070 nel Poulton in Pulford, Cheshire, England, il suo padre, Roger "Pincerna" D'Aubigny De St. Martin-Aubigny, aveva 36 anni e il suo madre, Adeliza De Mowbray, aveva 27 anni. Lui avuto almeno 2 figli con Margot Avaranches. Morì dopo il 1110, nel sua cittadina, e fu sepolto a Poulton in Pulford, Cheshire, England.

ja.Saint-Sauveur, Manche, Lower Normandy, F: フランスバセ-ノーマンディマンチェサイント-サウベウル
ja.Poulton in Pulford, Cheshire, England: イギリスチェシュアイアポウルトン イン プウルフォード
ja.lifeSummary: Richard Pincerna le Botelerが約1070年にイギリスチェシュアイアポウルトン イン プウルフォードで生まれた時，父Roger "Pincerna" D'Aubigny De St. Martin-Aubignyは36歳，母Adeliza De Mowbrayは27歳であった。彼はMargot Avaranchesとの間に少なくとも2人の息子をもうけた。彼は1110年以後に故郷で死去し，イギリスチェシュアイアポウルトン イン プウルフォードにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Pincerna le Boteler(이)가 대략 1070년 영국 체실르 파우턴 인 풀퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Roger "Pincerna" D'Aubigny De St. Martin-Aubigny(은)는 36세였고, 그의 어머니 Adeliza De Mowbray(은)는 27세였다. 그는 Margot Avaranches(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1110년 이후 그의 고향에서 사망했으며 영국 체실르 파우턴 인 풀퍼드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Pincerna le Boteler nasceu aproximadamente 1070, em Poulton in Pulford, Cheshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Roger "Pincerna" D'Aubigny De St. Martin-Aubigny, tinha 36 anos e sua mãe, Adeliza De Mowbray, tinha 27 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 2 filhos com Margot Avaranches. Ele faleceu após 1110, em sua cidade natal, e foi sepultado em Poulton in Pulford, Cheshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Pincerna le Boteler родился около 1070 г. в местности поултон ин пулфорд, чешир, Англия, его отцу по имени Roger "Pincerna" D'Aubigny De St. Martin-Aubigny было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Adeliza De Mowbray, 27. У него было по крайней мере 2 сына от Margot Avaranches. Он умер после 1110 г. в его родном городе и был похоронен в местности поултон ин пулфорд, чешир, Англия.

sm.Abt 1070–Aft 1119: e tusa o le 1070–ina ua ma'ea le 1110

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard Pincerna le Boteler nació aproximadamente 1070, en Poulton in Pulford, Cheshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Roger "Pincerna" D'Aubigny De St. Martin-Aubigny, tenía 36 años y su mamá, Adeliza De Mowbray, tenía 27 años. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos con Margot Avaranches. Murió después de 1110, en su pueblo, y fue sepultado en Poulton in Pulford, Cheshire, Inglaterra.
